behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing, and errors list all missing fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is validator-to-pipeline handoff (must stop downstream). REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejected Unit because behavior explicitly requires 'before any downstream processing', which is cross-module orchestration, not only local field-check logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake with missing name and insurance ID using stubbed downstream services (insurance/triage/scheduler/confirm). Assert error lists both missing fields and assert no downstream module was invoked.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: pure validation rule in one boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text is only input-rule evaluation, with no module handoff requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Call DOB validator directly with representative invalid values and assert specific validation failures.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility sends correct patient ID/plan code to payer API and maps eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior is about actual API request/response contract; real HTTP boundary can fail via serialization/header/path mismatches that mocks can hide (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration because behavior text says 'to the payer API', i.e., external API contract at transport boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real HTTP test server, execute eligibility check and assert on-wire payload fields and mapping of three API responses to internal statuses.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility becomes pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unreachable API is a network/timeout/connection failure mode; transport-level failures may differ from mocked exceptions (MOCK-HIDE). Also checks continuation across modules. Rejected Integration because concrete behavior text is specifically 'payer API is unreachable'.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real connection failure/timeout to payer endpoint, run intake, assert eligibility='pending' and pipeline proceeds to later steps.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is wrapper/orchestration correctness around model call payload construction, not model judgment. LLM-DEP disqualified by text: it checks inclusion of inputs/rubric in payload, not output quality. REAL-INFRA not required with stub client.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with stub model client capturing request; assert payload contains exact symptoms and rubric fields.

behaviors.description: Live AI symptom triage marks emergency symptom combinations as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model medical reasoning output; stubbed responses would always pass and hide misclassification. Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly about live AI assigning urgency for clinical symptom combinations.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated live triage calls for emergency symptom scenarios and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores clinician-readable explanation, not just numeric score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur in parser-to-storage handoff (field dropped/mapped incorrectly). No real infra or model-quality dependence required if using deterministic triage fixture. Rejected Unit because behavior includes persistence ('stores'), crossing module boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response containing score+explanation through parser and persistence layer; assert stored record includes non-empty explanation field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific with symptom-based rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is output-quality/grounding behavior from real model generation. Deterministic stubs cannot validate specificity quality. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires evaluating live explanation content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on multiple symptom profiles; assert explanations reference input-specific symptoms and provide urgency rationale, with low generic-template rate.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (same-day for high, within 48h for medium).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is scheduling logic + calendar data access (module wiring/local DB). REAL-INFRA OS/network not required; LLM-DEP none. Rejected Unit because 'first available slot' with SLA requires interaction with availability store.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with slot inventory, run scheduler for high/medium urgencies, assert chosen slot is earliest satisfying each SLA window.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: race condition appears under concurrent access across scheduler + shared DB transaction/locking. Still local DB/module interaction, not necessarily OS/network external. Rejected System because behavior text is about shared calendar DB concurrency, which can be reproduced in-process with real local DB integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same slot in a local transactional DB; assert at most one appointment is created for that slot.

behaviors.description: Confirmation includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primarily message composition/rendering rules in one boundary. No required real infra or model output dependence. Rejected Integration because behavior text checks content fields, not delivery channel or cross-service side effects.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation formatter with fixtures for visit types; assert rendered message contains required fields and correct prep instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate→insurance→triage→schedule→confirm) completes with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly deterministic stubbed triage disqualifies Agentic. Behavior is cross-module orchestration/state handoff, so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA not required if dependencies are local stubs/fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full intake pipeline with stubbed insurance and triage outputs plus local scheduler/confirm modules; assert stage order, state propagation, and successful completion.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes live AI triage call; LLM-DEP applies because real model output influences downstream urgency/scheduling. Also spans real external interactions, but higher-risk minimum is Agentic for model-dependent correctness. Rejected Workflow because behavior specifies one AI triage decision in an otherwise deterministic pipeline, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake against live payer integration and live triage model for a real submission fixture; assert unattended completion through confirmation with valid persisted artifacts.
